preview_text:
The model favors Colombia with a 61.4% win probability, while Uzbekistan is given a 13.0% chance of victory. The underdog faces a substantial gap: Colombia's forecast is more than four times higher than Uzbekistan's, reflecting a clear asymmetry in expected quality.

The draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome at 25.6%, suggesting the match carries meaningful competitive tension despite Colombia's advantage. The win probabilities are separated by roughly 48 percentage points, indicating a one-sided fixture, yet the elevated draw probability moderates the sense of inevitability. A tightly contested result remains plausible, even if Colombia enters as the heavy favorite.

The sportsbook consensus tilts further toward Colombia, pricing the win at 68.6% against the model's 61.4%—a 7-percentage-point divergence. The books compress the draw to 20.5%, while Uzbekistan's chance narrows to 10.9%. Polymarket prices align more closely with the sportsbooks than with the model. These gaps suggest the market is more confident in Colombian dominance than the statistical forecast; the dashboard does not adjudicate which view is justified.
